key_insight: The paper proposes reusable logical catalyst states in surface code that enable exact fine dyadic phase rotations via phase kickback, removing online Clifford+T synthesis error and making the non-Clifford depth constant.
engineering_relevance: This gives a fault-tolerant route to fixed-angle logical phase gates in codes with limited transversal options, which is useful for reducing overhead in quantum algorithms and hardware-constrained architectures.
method: Cultivate a logical catalyst as an eigenstate of a high-period Clifford circuit U using surface-code logical checks, syndrome extraction, postselection, and code growth.
Use a controlled-U gadget to implement Z^{2^{-b}} exactly by phase kickback, with a direct construction scaling as O(2^b) logical qubits.
Demonstrate a concrete sqrt(T)=Z^{1/8} catalyst using a nine-qubit brickwork Clifford circuit and controlled-CNOTs.
evidence_signal: Hybrid tensor-network plus stabilizer simulations report that at physical error rate p=10^{-3}, the catalyst can be grown to distance-seven rotated-surface-code blocks with logical leakage around 10^{-6} using about seven expected attempts.
watch_item: The approach relies on offline postselected catalyst cultivation and a specific controlled-U construction, so resource overhead and circuit design may still be significant for larger dyadic phases.
paper_title: Cultivating logical catalysts for fault-tolerant dyadic phase rotations
